title: rubus × spurius
intro: Rubus × spurius, also known as the black raspberry or black thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species that combines the characteristics of both black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus). The medicinal properties of Rubus × spurius include its high antioxidant content, anti-inflammatory properties, and ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and viruses. The health benefits of consuming Rubus × spurius include its potential to reduce the risk of heart disease, certain types of cancer, and cognitive decline, as well as its ability to support digestive health and immune function. Rubus × spurius can be prepared as a herbal remedy through various methods, including tea infusions, tinctures, and jam or jelly formulations.
